title: Lesson 5 — Money and Means

words.definition: severe or plain; without comfort, luxury, or adornment
words.example: The cabin was austere: one chair, one lamp, and bare walls.

words.definition: practicing severe self-discipline and abstaining from pleasure
words.example: The monk led an ascetic life of fasting and silence.

words.definition: sparing and economical with money or food
words.example: Frugal to the core, she darned socks rather than buy new ones.

words.definition: unwilling to spend money or resources; excessively stingy
words.example: His parsimonious boss refused to replace the broken chairs.
A munificent donor funded the entire new wing.

words.definition: spending money or resources recklessly and wastefully
words.example: His prodigal spending drained the inheritance in a year.

words.definition: recklessly extravagant or wasteful with money
words.example: The profligate heir gambled away the family estate.

words.definition: to waste money, time, or an opportunity carelessly
words.example: They squandered a two-goal lead in ten minutes.

words.definition: sumptuous and extravagant; given or spent in great amounts
words.example: A lavish banquet with a dozen courses awaited them.

words.definition: lacking in quantity or quality; scanty
words.example: A meager salary that barely covered the rent.
A long illness reduced the family to penury.

words.definition: generosity in giving; money or gifts given generously
words.example: The whole town depended on the tycoon's largess.

story: Old Mr. Ashford lived an [[austere|austere]], almost [[ascetic|ascetic]] life, so [[frugal|frugal]] that his neighbors called him [[parsimonious|parsimonious]]. Yet his will proved surprisingly [[munificent|munificent]]. His two nephews could not have differed more. The elder was [[prodigal|prodigal]] and [[profligate|profligate]], quick to [[squander|squander]] any windfall on [[lavish|lavish]] parties; the younger, raised in near-[[penury|penury]] after his own father's ruin, had learned to stretch a [[meager|meager]] wage across a month. The family lawyer waited to see which man the old miser's sudden [[largess|largess]] would rescue — and which would simply waste it.
